jokes.text: Like 'get away with murdering my first wife' kind of dough.
jokes.setup: Floyd describing his promotion and salary increase
jokes.punchline: Casually comparing money to what you'd need for murder cover-up
jokes.explanation: Dark humor with perfect casual delivery. The specificity of 'first wife' adds to the absurdity.

jokes.text: You could get that third humidifier you always dreamed about.
jokes.setup: Floyd talking about hanging a huge TV
jokes.punchline: Liz's mundane counter-suggestion about humidifiers
jokes.explanation: Perfect Liz character moment - practical and unsexy response to romantic gesture

jokes.text: My son Ahmed will keep his motorcycles here.
jokes.setup: Floyd deciding to take the apartment
jokes.punchline: Landlord casually mentioning son storing motorcycles
jokes.explanation: Random absurd detail that deflates Floyd's excitement about the apartment

jokes.text: I have hollow bones. Like a bird.
jokes.setup: Phoebe introducing herself to Liz
jokes.punchline: Randomly mentioning having hollow bones
jokes.explanation: Perfectly weird character introduction. The specificity and casual delivery make it memorable.

jokes.text: We've just won the auction rights to a fantastic collection of Chinese erotica.
jokes.setup: Phoebe saying she should go
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of Chinese erotica business
jokes.explanation: Absurd wealthy person business delivered matter-of-factly

jokes.text: And now he's a post-op transgender.
jokes.setup: Jack's Princeton roommate's marriage failed after 8 months
jokes.punchline: Unexpected escalation to gender transition
jokes.explanation: Feels like cheap shock value rather than earned comedy. Doesn't fit character well.

jokes.text: It's...made of gold.
jokes.setup: Liz commenting that the credit card is heavy
jokes.punchline: Jack's matter-of-fact explanation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack moment - casual wealth displayed through deadpan delivery

jokes.text: It's gonna be at least $30 million with all the Claymation sex scenes in it.
jokes.setup: Tracy explaining his Thomas Jefferson movie needs a loan
jokes.punchline: Casually mentioning Claymation sex scenes
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Tracy absurdity - the specificity of 'Claymation' makes it funnier than just 'sex scenes'

jokes.text: I'm gonna have so much money, my grandkids are gonna play lacrosse.
jokes.setup: Tracy talking about his comeback plan
jokes.punchline: Lacrosse as the ultimate symbol of wealth
jokes.explanation: Perfect comedy writing - specific, character-driven, and culturally observant

jokes.text: You're never gonna lose a job to a guy named Garkel.
jokes.setup: Kenneth reassuring Floyd about the job competition
jokes.punchline: Name-based discrimination as career advice
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the immediate payoff when Garkel appears

jokes.text: Did someone say my name?
jokes.setup: Kenneth just said Garkel can't win because of his name
jokes.punchline: Perfect comedic timing of Garkel's entrance
jokes.explanation: Classic sitcom timing joke - simple but perfectly executed

jokes.text: Well-played, Garkel.
jokes.setup: Garkel's perfectly timed entrance
jokes.punchline: Floyd's resigned acknowledgment of being outmaneuvered
jokes.explanation: Nice capper to the Garkel sequence - Floyd accepting defeat gracefully

jokes.text: No, I like Elmer Fudd. Kill da wabbit
jokes.setup: Asked if she likes Wagner
jokes.punchline: Mishearing as Elmer Fudd cartoon reference
jokes.explanation: Classic Liz lowbrow vs highbrow moment - fits character perfectly

jokes.text: Just a...whirl of wind.
jokes.setup: Phoebe mentions whirlwind romance
jokes.punchline: Liz awkwardly breaking down the metaphor
jokes.explanation: Typical Liz awkward social moment - overthinking simple phrases

jokes.text: I would have a three-way with two Jacks.
jokes.setup: Liz saying she understands why Phoebe would marry Jack
jokes.punchline: Escalating to inappropriate sexual fantasy
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe comedy - Liz oversharing in trying to be supportive

jokes.text: I'm Samantha, you're Charlotte, and you're the lady at home who watches it.
jokes.setup: Liz asking how Sex and the City they are
jokes.punchline: Jenna assigning roles, with Phoebe as the audience
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta joke - Jenna's narcissism and the dig at Phoebe perfectly executed

jokes.text: Oh, Tracy, you can't use any of my songs.
jokes.setup: Michael McDonald denying permission
jokes.punchline: Kenneth's Michael McDonald impression
jokes.explanation: Kenneth doing impressions fits his character and provides comic relief

jokes.text: The Black Crusaders.
jokes.setup: Tracy thinking powerful forces are against him
jokes.punchline: Naming the conspiracy 'Black Crusaders'
jokes.explanation: Sets up major recurring bit in episode - perfect Tracy paranoia

jokes.text: If I was gonna spend $600 to have my boobs pinched, I would have gone to that fundraiser at the Clintons' house.
jokes.setup: Looking at expensive bra
jokes.punchline: Alternative expensive boob-pinching opportunity
jokes.explanation: Clever political humor with unexpected comparison

jokes.text: Yeah, you gotta talk to your landlord about that rat race.
jokes.setup: Liz complaining about being tired of the rat race
jokes.punchline: Floyd taking it literally about actual rats
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay moment that reveals Floyd heard actual racing

jokes.text: That is also a place.
jokes.setup: Liz suggesting Cleveland as vacation spot
jokes.punchline: Floyd's underwhelmed acknowledgment
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan reaction to Cleveland as destination

jokes.text: That is also a place.
jokes.setup: Floyd mentions Cleveland as alternative to Vermont or Bahamas
jokes.punchline: Liz's underwhelming acknowledgment of Cleveland
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan response. Great economy and captures universal attitude toward Cleveland.

jokes.text: Oh, there is nothing like New York in the spring! It's--oh!
jokes.setup: Woman celebrating New York in spring
jokes.punchline: Gets hit by something immediately after
jokes.explanation: Classic ironic timing - praising NYC right before getting attacked by it

jokes.text: He spit in my mouth!
jokes.setup: Man apparently spitting at them
jokes.punchline: Specific gross detail of where it landed
jokes.explanation: Gross-out humor that motivates the Cleveland escape

jokes.text: Bill Cosby and Oprah Winfrey are the chief majors. But Jesse Jackson, Colin Powell, and Gordon from Sesame Street, they're members too.
jokes.setup: Explaining the Black Crusaders conspiracy
jokes.punchline: Including Gordon from Sesame Street with real celebrities
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation - mixing real and fictional characters in conspiracy shows Tracy's mindset perfectly

jokes.text: And they meet four times a year in the skull of the Statue of Liberty.
jokes.setup: Describing Black Crusaders meeting
jokes.punchline: Impossible meeting location
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy conspiracy theory detail - specific and impossible

jokes.text: They tanked 50 Cent's movie. They blew out Terrell Owens' ankle. And they canceled Eddie Murphy's Oscar, 'cause he, uh, ran out on Scary Spice.
jokes.setup: Examples of Black Crusaders' power
jokes.punchline: Increasingly ridiculous and specific 'evidence'
jokes.explanation: Great conspiracy theory escalation with specific pop culture references

jokes.text: Tracy Jordan, the Black Crusaders are coming for you.
jokes.setup: Tracy watching TV news
jokes.punchline: News seeming to address him directly
jokes.explanation: Good paranoia escalation - TV seeming to confirm his conspiracy

jokes.text: I haven't been above 72nd Street in over a decade.
jokes.setup: Phoebe speaking at Columbia University
jokes.punchline: Jack's admission about geographic limitations
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack character detail - elitist Manhattan geography snobbery

jokes.text: wolf-like. Lupine.
jokes.setup: Describing attraction to Phoebe
jokes.punchline: Unnecessarily adding the Latin term
jokes.explanation: Classic Jack pretentiousness - adding unnecessary Latin

jokes.text: It's an anagram for 'Outlaw Sour Tray.'
jokes.setup: Tracy showing Oprah magazine cover
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous anagram that makes no sense
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy paranoia - seeing messages in random anagrams

jokes.text: They want me to disappear like Coolio. Coolio is around!
jokes.setup: Tracy saying Black Crusaders made Coolio disappear
jokes.punchline: Floyd pointing out Coolio still exists
jokes.explanation: Good reality check moment - Floyd being the voice of reason

jokes.text: Cleveland tourism jingle
jokes.setup: Arriving in Cleveland
jokes.punchline: Overly enthusiastic tourism promotion
jokes.explanation: Standard tourism parody - competent but not exceptional

jokes.text: Oh, you are so skinny. You really should eat something.
jokes.setup: Woman asking if Liz is a model
jokes.punchline: Midwest concern about Liz being too thin
jokes.explanation: Good Midwest vs NYC cultural difference moment

jokes.text: Tracy Jordan, you've gotta get away
jokes.setup: Cleveland tourism song continuing
jokes.punchline: Song seeming to address Tracy specifically
jokes.explanation: Good continuation of Tracy's paranoia - even Cleveland seems after him

jokes.text: We need a host for a new cooking show on WKYC.
jokes.setup: Floyd suggesting Liz could find work in Cleveland
jokes.punchline: Immediate job offer for completely different career
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment - Cleveland's lower standards presented as opportunity

jokes.text: For God sakes, Lemon, we'd all like to flee to the Cleve.
jokes.setup: Liz explaining she was in Cleveland
jokes.punchline: Jack's nickname and understanding of Cleveland's appeal
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack moment - 'the Cleve' nickname and his unexpected relatability

jokes.text: For God sakes, Lemon, we'd all like to flee to the Cleve. And club up down at the Flats and have lunch with Little Richard.
jokes.setup: Jack responding to Liz being in Cleveland
jokes.punchline: Referencing her specific Cleveland experiences
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback with Jack's typical dramatic delivery

jokes.text: Yeah, we're all models west of the Allegheny.
jokes.setup: Liz saying in Cleveland she's a model
jokes.punchline: Jack's geographical explanation for beauty standards
jokes.explanation: Brilliant geographic joke - specific mountain reference makes it funnier

jokes.text: Well, I'm Jack's Yoko.
jokes.setup: Phoebe comparing herself to Yoko Ono's influence on Lennon
jokes.punchline: Wanting to be compared to famously divisive figure
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Phoebe not understanding the negative connotations

jokes.text: What happened to your accent? Um... I don't know what you're on about. You...daft wanker.
jokes.setup: Liz catching Phoebe's accent slipping
jokes.punchline: Phoebe overcompensating with exaggerated British terms
jokes.explanation: Good reveal moment - fake accent exposed and overcompensation

jokes.text: Liz... I took that job.
jokes.setup: Liz explaining she can't move to Cleveland
jokes.punchline: Floyd revealing he already committed
jokes.explanation: Perfect dramatic timing - Floyd's revelation creates maximum awkwardness

jokes.text: Lemon, Phoebe was right. You are infatuated with me.
jokes.setup: Liz's emotional outburst about Phoebe
jokes.punchline: Jack completely misinterpreting her motives
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack narcissism - completely missing the point

jokes.text: In Paris, France.
jokes.setup: Liz mentioning Jack fell asleep on Phoebe in Paris
jokes.punchline: Jack clarifying which Paris with wounded pride
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack wounded pride moment - the clarification shows his embarrassment
